rationale: One-time external consulting, integration, and parallel-run costs incurred during ERP rollout; not expected to recur post go-live.
Severance and retention payments tied to consolidation of customer success teams; ongoing cost base expected to normalize to steady-state headcount.

description: Non-recurring legal settlement and outside counsel
rationale: Settlement and associated legal fees related to a single legacy customer dispute; management asserts no similar matters are recurring.
Professional fees tied to the current sale process and financing diligence; not part of ongoing operating cost structure.

description: Data security incident remediation and customer credits
rationale: Costs and credits associated with a discrete security incident; remediation program completed and costs are not expected to continue at current level.
Retention/transaction bonuses triggered by the contemplated transaction; should be treated as deal-related and excluded from run-rate.

description: Temporary contractor spend to clear implementation backlog
rationale: Short-term contractor program used to address implementation backlog following FY25 onboarding surge; expected to unwind as utilization normalizes.

description: Bad debt spike on one distressed customer; normalize to historical loss rate
rationale: Incremental provision associated with one distressed customer write-off; baseline bad debt remains in run-rate, but excess is treated as non-recurring.

description: Related-party hosting / managed services markup above market
rationale: Current hosting services are provided under a related-party arrangement with a markup; adjustment reflects expected cost at market rates post-transition.

description: Insurance proceeds recorded in other income
rationale: Non-recurring insurance recovery recognized in earnings; removed to avoid overstatement of sustainable profitability.

description: Revenue cut-off on milestone-based services recognized early
rationale: Timing-related revenue recognition on select milestone implementations appears accelerated; adjustment defers revenue and associated margin to delivery periods.

description: Excess capitalization of software development costs above policy baseline
rationale: Higher-than-historical capitalization rate reduces reported operating expense; adjustment reflects a normalized capitalization ratio consistent with policy and prior periods.

description: Under-accrual of annual SaaS platform fees
rationale: Annual platform/vendor fees appear under-accrued versus contract schedule; adjustment increases run-rate expense to contractual levels.
